revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 浅間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]敷根1（旧住所：本郷851ｰ1） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 6月1日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=浅間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''浅間神社'''（せんげんじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402102 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == [[伊豆急下田駅]]の北西至近にある山「[[下田富士]]（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の[[国道136号]]沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[木花開耶姫]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402102 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:せんけんしんしや しもたし}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 浅間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]敷根1（旧住所：本郷851ｰ1） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 下田(富士)浅間神社、本郷(富士)浅間神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 6月1日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=浅間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''浅間神社'''（せんげんじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402102 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
地名を冠して、'''下田(富士)浅間神社'''（しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）、'''本郷(富士)浅間神社'''（ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）等とも称される。
== 概要 == [[伊豆急下田駅]]の北西至近にある山「[[下田富士]]（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の[[国道136号]]沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[木花開耶姫]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|浅間神社 (下田市)}}

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|浅間神社 (下田市)}}

{{Infobox religious building

| mapframe = yes

{{nihongo|'''Sengen Shrine (Shimoda)'''|浅間神社 (下田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 4 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 浅間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]敷根1（旧住所：本郷851ｰ1） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫]] |社格 = [[式内社]]論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 下田(富士)浅間神社、本郷(富士)浅間神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 6月1日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=浅間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''浅間神社'''（せんげんじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
地名を冠して、'''下田(富士)浅間神社'''（しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）、'''本郷(富士)浅間神社'''（ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）等とも称される。
== 概要 == [[伊豆急下田駅]]の北西至近にある山「[[下田富士]]（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の[[国道136号]]沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[木花開耶姫]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:せんけんしんしや しもたし}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:浅間神社]]

地名を冠して、'''下田(富士)浅間神社'''（しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）、'''本郷(富士)浅間神社'''（ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）等とも称される。
== 概要 == [[伊豆急下田駅]]の北西至近にある山「[[下田富士]]（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の[[国道136号]]沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[木花開耶姫]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
[[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:浅間神社]]
==End Japanese==

[[JA:浅間神社 (下田市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q134930730}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|浅間神社 (下田市)|version=103507360|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 浅間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}敷根1（旧住所：本郷851ｰ1） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|木花開耶姫}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 下田(富士)浅間神社、本郷(富士)浅間神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 6月1日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=浅間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''浅間神社'''（せんげんじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
地名を冠して、'''下田(富士)浅間神社'''（しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）、'''本郷(富士)浅間神社'''（ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）等とも称される。
== 概要 == {{jalink|伊豆急下田駅|Q6102244}}の北西至近にある山「{{jalink|下田富士}}（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の{{jalink|国道136号|Q11421406}}沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。
創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。
== 祭神 == * {{jalink|木花開耶姫}}
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:浅間神社]]

{{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''浅間神社'''（せんげんじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

地名を冠して、'''下田(富士)浅間神社'''（しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）、'''本郷(富士)浅間神社'''（ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ）等とも称される。

=== 概要 ===
{{jalink|伊豆急下田駅|Q6102244}}の北西至近にある山「{{jalink|下田富士}}（しもだふじ）」の頂上に鎮座しており、南側の{{jalink|国道136号|Q11421406}}沿いの参道（登山道）から上がって行く形になる<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html 下田富士] - 伊豆ジオ遺産</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm 浅間神社] - お祭り三昧</ref>。

創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''意波与命神社'''（おはよのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 いはよ姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="om"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|木花開耶姫}}

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 浅間神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{デフォルトソート:せんけんしんしや しもたし}}

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{jalink|Shimoda Fuji}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{jalink|Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{jalink|National Route 136|Q11421406}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. It is also recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{Default sort: Sengen Shrine, Shimoda City}}

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{jalink|Shimoda Fuji}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{jalink|Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{jalink|National Route 136|Q11421406}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. It is also recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{Default sort: Sengen Shrine, Shimoda City}}

|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sengen Shrine|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|40|56.8|N|138|56|27.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{jalink|Shimoda Fuji}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{jalink|Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{jalink|National Route 136|Q11421406}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. It is also recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{Default sort: Sengen Shrine, Shimoda City}}

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimoda Fuji|1=UNKNOWN}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{ill|lt=Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244|WD=Q6102244}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{ill|lt=National Route 136|Q11421406|WD=Q11421406}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimoda Fuji|1=UNKNOWN}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{ill|lt=Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244|WD=Q6102244|1=Izukyū Shimoda Station}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{ill|lt=National Route 136|Q11421406|WD=Q11421406|1=Japan National Route 136}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is also recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimoda Fuji|1=UNKNOWN}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{ill|lt=Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244|WD=Q6102244|1=Izukyū Shimoda Station}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{ill|lt=National Route 136|Q11421406|WD=Q11421406|1=Japan National Route 136}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is also recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{Default sort: Sengen Shrine, Shimoda City}}

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimoda Fuji|1=UNKNOWN}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{ill|lt=Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244|WD=Q6102244|1=Izukyū Shimoda Station}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{ill|lt=National Route 136|Q11421406|WD=Q11421406|1=Japan National Route 136}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is also recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

'''Sengen Shrine''' (せんげんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401057 Sengen Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

Named after its locality, it is also known as '''Shimoda (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (しもだ(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ) and '''Hongo (Fuji) Sengen Shrine''' (ほんごう(ふじ)せんげんじんじゃ), among other names.

=== Overview ===
The shrine is enshrined at the summit of the mountain called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimoda Fuji|1=UNKNOWN}}" (しもだふじ), located very close to the northwest of {{ill|lt=Izukyu-Shimoda Station|Q6102244|WD=Q6102244|1=Izukyū Shimoda Station}}. Visitors ascend via the approach (mountain trail) that begins along {{ill|lt=National Route 136|Q11421406|WD=Q11421406|1=Japan National Route 136}} on the southern side.<ref>[https://izugeo13.sakuraweb.com/S-028-fuji.html Shimoda Fuji] – Izu Geo Heritage</ref><ref name="om">[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/simoda/sengen.htm Sengen Shrine] – Omatsuri Zanmai</ref>

Although the date of its founding is unknown, it is an ancient shrine that is considered to correspond to '''Ohayo-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (おはよのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is also recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade: Ihayo-hime no Myōjin.”<ref name="om"/>
